name: MAS Boston Society
mission: Tarbiya Department Tarbiya aims at rooting the faith, purifying the soul, expanding and refining knowledge/understanding, developing and refining all aspects of character, boosting commitment, building skills, and instilling the discipline necessary to conduct a successful, fulfilling, meaningful, and balanced life of learning, virtue, commitment, and service of Islam and humanity.
theoryOfChange: Developing God-conscious community servants who can benefit all of society through spiritual training and civic engagement.

grantsData.purpose: MONEY COLLECTED AS PART OF ZAKAT (AN EXPECTATION IN THE MUSLIM FAITH THAT ALL FINANCIALLY STABLE MUSLIMS PAY MONEY TOWARDS HELPING NEEDY INDIVIDUALS IN THE COMMUNITY) IS GIVEN TO THE ISLAMIC CIRCLE OF NORTH AMERICA (ICNA) RELIEF, WHO THEN DISTRIBUTES THIS MONEY BASED ON AN APPLICATION AND REVIEW PROCESS TO NEEDY INDIVIDUALS.

PAYMENT TO ISB (A RELIGOUS ORGANIZATION) FOR ISB TO PAYDOWN LOAN WHICH WAS USED TO FINANCE THE CONTRUCTION OF THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF BOSTON CULTURAL CENTER (THE CENTER). THIS IS CONSISTENT WITH THE MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT BETWEEN MAS BOSTON AND ISB FOR THE CENTER.

MONEY COLLECTED AS PART OF ZAKAT (AN EXPECTATION IN THE MUSLIM FAITH THAT ALL FINANCIALLY STABLE MUSLIMS PAY MONEY TOWARDS HELPING NEEDY INDIVIDUALS IN THE COMMUNITY) IS GIVEN TO THE ISLAMIC CIRCLE OF NORTH AMERICA (ICNA) RELIEF, WHO THEN DISTRIBUTES THIS MONEY BASED ON AN APPLICATION AND REVIEW PROCESS TO NEEDY INDIVIDUALS.
PAYMENT TO ISB FOR ISB TO PAY DOWN THE CULTURAL CENTER (MOSQUE) LOAN.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows high donor fit for the Muslim community by offering zakat-eligible programs and addressing urgent religious and cultural needs [1]. Its mission focuses on rooting faith and purifying the soul, which aligns with the spiritual priorities of its core constituency [4].
dimension_explanations.impact: MAS Boston demonstrates a strong commitment to its mission by maintaining a program expense ratio of 85.3% and a total revenue of $3,286,832 [2][4]. While the organization focuses on capacity building through its Tarbiya and youth programs, specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not publicly detailed [4].

baseline_narrative.headline: Muslim American Society - MAS Boston empowers the Muslim community through comprehensive spiritual development, youth leadership, and civic engagement.

Exceptional fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.05 per $1 raised

alignment.explanation: The organization is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="1">zakat-eligible giving</cite> and serving a <cite id="4">niche religious-cultural cause area</cite> that is often underserved.

credibility.explanation: Donors can find confidence in MAS Boston's <cite id="4">independent board governance</cite> and its long-standing presence in the Boston area since 2005. The organization maintains <cite id="4">detailed financial reporting</cite> through its Form 990 filings.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity could <cite id="2">publish annual impact reports</cite> that include audited financial summaries and specific beneficiary milestones.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value local community infrastructure and want to support holistic Islamic character development through a highly efficient, zakat-eligible regional institution.
donor_motivations: Want to support Muslim youth leadership in the West
Seek to fulfill zakat through established local religious institutions
Prioritize spiritual development (Tarbiya) as a core cause
giving_considerations: Donations support regional capacity rather than global emergency relief
Impact is measured through community engagement rather than RCTs
The organization acts as a hub, often regranting to other local partners
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-volume international humanitarian metrics or those who require rigorous academic impact evaluations.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="4">86.0% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core mission and community programs.
MAS Boston demonstrates lean operations by spending only <cite id="2">$0.05 per $1 raised</cite> in fundraising costs.
The organization maintains a <cite id="1">clear zakat policy</cite> supporting multiple asnaf categories including the poor and wayfarers.

strengths_deep_dive: MAS Boston's financial health is anchored by its ability to maintain high program spending while achieving a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 13.4%</cite>, indicating steady growth in community support. Its grantmaking activity also highlights its role as a community pillar, providing <cite id="4">$332,783 in domestic grants</cite> to partner organizations like the Islamic Society of Boston. This collaborative approach is bolstered by <cite id="2">3.2 months of working capital</cite>, providing a resilient buffer for its regional operations.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Society (MAS) Boston is a community-focused institution dedicated to fostering spiritual growth and civic leadership within the Muslim community of Greater Boston. The organization operates with a mission centered on <cite id="4">Tarbiya, which aims at rooting faith</cite> and purifying the soul through structured character development. MAS Boston manages a diverse portfolio of services, including <cite id="1">youth development, public affairs, and convert support programs</cite>. Financially, the organization demonstrates significant scale for a regional religious entity, reporting <cite id="4">total revenue of $3,286,832</cite> in its most recent comprehensive filing. A defining characteristic of its operations is its high level of fiscal efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="2">program expense ratio of 85.3%</cite>, which ensures that the vast majority of donor contributions are directed toward community services rather than administrative overhead. Furthermore, the organization is highly efficient in its resource acquisition, spending only <cite id="2">$0.05 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. As a <cite id="1">zakat-eligible organization</cite>, MAS Boston provides a vital channel for Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations, specifically serving categories such as the <cite id="1">poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin)</cite>.
